title: phelipanche purpurea subsp. millefolii
intro: Phelipanche purpurea subsp. millefolii, also known as purple broomrape, is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants, typically those in the legume family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Phelipanche purpurea subsp. millefolii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems.
